summary: Clamp the post-429 halved rate to `l.floor` (the user-configured starting rate) instead of the hard-coded 0.5.
reason: Greptile review (P1) flagged that for `--rate-limit < 0.5`, `OnRateLimit` divided the rate by 2 and then clamped UP to 0.5 — making a 429 *increase* the rate above the user's intended ceiling. Using `l.floor` preserves user intent: a 429 can never push the rate above where it started. Required updating two unit tests in `cliutil_test.go` that encoded the old buggy semantics (`TestAdaptiveLimiter_HalvesOnRateLimit` and renamed `TestAdaptiveLimiter_FloorsAtUserConfiguredFloor`). Originally deferred to upstream; applied locally for the same reason as the TOCTOU fix above.
validated_outcome: Updated tests pass; `go test ./...` clean across all packages.
upstream_issue: TBD — same generator-template fix as p2-toctou-window.
